scene_description:
## Google Street View Image Description

### FOREGROUND

- **Road surface**: Light gray asphalt road with visible cracks and faint tire marks in the center. The road is a single lane in each direction with no clear lane markings. The road surface appears slightly worn in spots. (center foreground)
- **Curb**: Light gray concrete curb on both sides of the road with visible cracks and wear. The left curb has more significant damage and visible grass growing through cracks. (left and right foreground)
- **Building (left side)**: Two-story structure with red lower level (painted) and white upper level. The red section has a terracotta tile roof with visible wear on edges. (left foreground)
- **Building entrance (left)**: Black metal gate with vertical bars covering the ground-level entrance. A small concrete step in front of the gate. (left foreground)
- **House number**: "256" painted in black on the red building facade. (left foreground)
- **Circular objects**: Two circular items on the red wall above the number "256" - one appears to be a metal plate with an indistinct logo, the other a plain gold circle. (left foreground)
- **Blue car**: Partial front of a blue sedan visible on the right edge of the image (only the hood and front bumper are visible). (right foreground)
- **Small vegetation**: Patches of grass and weeds growing from cracks along the left sidewalk. (left foreground)

### MIDGROUND

- **Buildings (left side)**: Two-story buildings continuing along the street with similar white upper levels and red lower levels. Some have white roll-down doors, some have black metal gates. (left midground)
- **Buildings (right side)**: White and red buildings with closed doors and metal gates along the right side of the street. (right midground)
- **House number**: "279" painted in black on the red facade of a building on the right side. (right midground)
- **Sidewalks**: Narrow concrete sidewalks on both sides, showing some wear and patchy grass growth. (left and right midground)
- **Utility poles**: Multiple wooden utility poles visible along the street, with many black power lines crisscrossing overhead. (various positions along the street)
- **Palm trees**: Several palm trees visible in the distance at the end of the street. (center background)

### BACKGROUND

- **Distant buildings**: More buildings with red and white facades extending down the street. (center background)
- **Power lines**: Numerous black power lines crisscrossing from multiple utility poles. (throughout the image, above the street)
- **Sky**: Partly cloudy sky with scattered white clouds. (upper portion of image)
- **Additional vehicles**: A white vehicle partially visible in the distance on the left side of the street. (left background)

### ROAD & TRAFFIC FEATURES

- **Driving side**: The blue car is positioned on the right side of the road, indicating right-hand traffic.
- **Curb**: The curbs on both sides are light gray concrete, with some visible discoloration and weathering. 
- **Road surface condition**: The asphalt shows some wear, cracks, and patches with no visible lane markings or crosswalks.
- **No road name signs or street name signs** are visible in the image.

details.name: house number
details.reason: Numerical digits on building facades can help identify specific street numbering systems.
details.focused_description: The house number "256" is visible, written in black, handwritten-style numerals on a red wall. It is positioned next to two circular objects (one light-colored, one dark-colored) on the building's exterior. The number appears slightly faded but legible.

details.name: metal plate
details.reason: A logo on a metal plate can serve as a highly specific regional or even local identifier.
details.focused_description: The metal plate is circular in shape. Its central area is a muted greenish-brown color with faint, indistinct markings. The outer rim features a dark (blue/purple) decorative pattern. The material appears metallic, showing signs of wear or aging with discoloration. No legible text is visible.

details.name: wooden utility poles
details.reason: Wooden poles with dense, crisscrossing power lines are common in many developing regions.
details.focused_description: A tall cylindrical wooden utility pole stands vertically. It is made of weathered wood with a natural brown color, showing signs of aging. The pole is upright and has electrical insulators and wires attached to it. No text is visible on the pole.

details.name: terracotta tile roof
details.reason: Terracotta tiles are characteristic of Mediterranean, Latin American, and some Asian architectures.
details.focused_description: A terracotta tile roof with reddish-brown ceramic tiles arranged in a sloped, overlapping pattern. The tiles form a continuous angled surface under the building’s white upper structure. The material is terracotta ceramic, and the tiles appear intact with no visible markings.
